content_en:
The Fiqh of Marriage in the Light of the Quran and Sunnah

Covering the Dower, Wedding Night, Wedding Feast and Rights of the Husband and Wife

Dr. Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan Professor, College of Shareeah Muhammad ibn Saud Islamic University Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

Translated by Jamaal al-Din M. Zarabozo

Printed at the Expense of the Late Qasim Darwish Fakhrou

‘The Figh of Marriage

INTRODUCTION TO THE SECOND EDITION ..ssscsssssessessssssssees

INTRODUCTION TO THE [FIRST EDITION] OF THE BOOK.

THE TYPES OF RIGHTS OF THE WIFE UPON HER HUSBAND. THE RULING CONCERNING STATING THE DOWER AT THE TIME OF THE MARRIAGE CONTRACT THE STATUS OF THE DOWER THE MAXIMUM AMOUNT OF DOWER ALLOWED THE MINIMUM AMOUNT ALLOWABLE FOR A DOWER. EXTRAVAGANT DOWERS: THE CAUSES, RESULTS AND RULING ‘WHEN THE WOMAN IS ENTITLED TO HER ENTIRE DOWER PAYING HALF OF THE DOWER.

CONTRACTING OF THE MARRIAGE ... CASES WHEREIN THE MAN IS NOT OBLIGED TO PAY ANY PORTION OF THE

THE RULING CONCERNING PAYING THE DOWER ON THE SPOT OR OVER TIME... THE RULING CONCERNING A PORTION OF THE DOWER PAID IN ADVANCE O ORA GIFT GIVEN TO THE BRIDE IN THE CASE WHERE THE MAN DECIDES NOT TO

MARRY THE WOMAN .. ENGAGEMENT RINGS: THEIR RULING FOR EITHER MEN OR WOMEN

TWO: ANNOUNCING THE MARRIAGE AND MAKING IT KNOWN . 69

THE RULING CONCERNING MAKING THE MARRIAGE KNOWN THE MEANING OF ANNOUNCING THE MARRIAGE...

‘The Figh of Marriage

THREE: THE WEDDING DINNER (AZ-WALEEMAH) AND AN ADMONITION CONCERNING EXTRAVAGANCE AT WEDDING PARTIES ....

THE ROOT OF THE WORD WALEEMAH, ITS LINGUISTIC AND TECHNICAL DEFINITION.

FOUR: CONGRATULATIONS UPON MARRIAGE AND SUPPLICATIONS FOR THE SPOUSE...

THE WISDOM BEHIND THE SANCTIONING OF CONGRATULATIONS... WHAT THE CONGRATULATING PERSON SHOULD SAY TO HIS BROTHER. THE HONEYMOON

FIVE: A WARNING AND A CURE ASI SEE IT

SIX: ETIQUETTE OF THE WEDDING NIGHT AND MARITAL

The Figh of Marriage

Tn the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Most Merciful

All praises are due to Allah; we praise Him; we seek His help; we seek His forgiveness; and we seek His guidance. We seek refuge in Allah from the evil in our souls and the badness of our deeds. For whomever Allah guides, there is none to lead him astray. And for whomever He allows to go astray, there is none to guide him. I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship except Allah, for whom there is no partner. And I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger.

The vast majority of Muslims will get married at one time or another in their lives, if they have not already done so. Marriage is definitely one of the most important events that takes place in one’s private life. However, like all other aspects of one’s life, one must be careful to follow the proper steps when it comes to marriage. One must be guided by the laws of Allah when it comes to the marriage proposal, seeing the prospective bride, the marriage contract, the marriage ceremony, the dower and so forth. Too many times these issues are ruled not by Islamic law but by the prevalent customs of the people. The customs have become the standard to such an extent that many people are not aware that some of those customs are in contradiction with what the Quran and sunnah have laid down as guidance for mankind. The situation has sometimes deteriorated to the point that when people are told of the true teachings of Islam concerning marriage, they reject it as something foreign and alien to what they have come to know as “Islam.”

It is imperative that all Muslims return to the Quran, sunnah and Islamic figh to guide them in matters related to marriage. Every aspect of a person’s life should be guided by the revelation and guidance that has come from Allah. This is true for everything from the way the government runs to how a person treats his or her own spouse. No action

‘The Figh of Marriage

falls outside of the watchful view of Allah and, hence, no action falls outside of the guidance of His religion.

Dr. Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan (may Allah reward him and preserve his life) has striven to remove much of the mistaken notions concerning married life in Islam. He has written three small but excellent works that cover some of the most important aspects of marriage. One of these works concerns marital discord (al-nushooz). A second work, of which a new edition is currently being prepared, deals with “pre-nuptial contracts” or conditions in the marriage contract. The third work, which is now before the reader, is a more general discussion of the basic aspects of the dower, the marriage ceremony and wedding party, and the rights of the husband and the wife. It is hoped that these works will allow Muslims to understand and implement the guidance of Islam related to marriage. In doing so, they will lead themselves by the grace and mercy of Allah to the best way of life in this world and to Allah’s pleasure in the Hereafter.

I would like to express my personal thanks to Shaikh Saalih alSadlaan for giving us the permission to translate this work and his other works. I would also like to thank Br. Homaidan al-Turki for his relentless efforts in getting these works published. I must also the sister, who prefers to remain anonymous, who edited and commented upon the manuscript. And last but not least, I would like to thank my beloved wife for her continual support, help and patience. May Allah reward all of them with a great reward in both this life and the Hereafter.

The translator, Jamaal al-Din M. Zarabozo

Boulder, CO January 25, 1999

The Figh of Marriage

Introduction to the Second Edition

All praise be to Allah who created for us spouses from our own selves and has placed between us love and mercy. That is one of His great signs.

Peace and blessings be upon the one to whom his Lord said, “We have sent messengers before you and We made for them wives and offspring” (a/-Raad 38), our Prophet Muhammad. Upon him be the best of blessings and most complete peace.

Since the issues related to married life, wedding feasts and consummation are among those issues for which people need explanations with shareeah evidence, I thought it good to write about what every Muslim should know concerning these matters.!

I have joined together two of my books in this research that is in front of you. Those works covered the dower, wedding party, manners related to the night of the wedding and the obligatory rights of each spouse. I have also added discussions clarifying some of the negative aspects and mistakes that some people commit concerning those topics.

This is the second edition of this book. The necessary corrections to the first edition were made. Care was also taken to discuss the sources of the hadith and to clarify matters that needed to be clarified. In addition, some new topics were discussed.

This is my striving and Allah is the one to be asked to make it beneficial when it goes back to Him. And He is the one to be asked to

1 We have written two other books related to marital issues. These are al-Shuroot al-Nikaab (“Conditions in the Marriage Contract”), which was completed in 1391 AH. and for which a second edition is under preparation, and al-Nushooz (“Marital Discord”) which was completed in 1412 AH. [By the race and mercy of Allah, alNushooz has been translated into English and published by al-Basheer Company for Publications and Translations. The other work, al-Shuroot, is a future project, Allah willing, of al-Basheer Company—JZ]

‘The Fight of Marriage

make it on the scales of my good deeds on the day in which wealth and children will not avail anyone except for the one who comes to Allah with a pure heart. And may the peace and blessings be upon our Prophet Muhammad, his family and all of his Companions.

Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan

(may Allah pardon him, his parents and all Muslims)

‘The Fight of Marriage

Introduction to the [First Edition! of the Book

There came over woman a period of time in which her situation was restless. Her affairs were not calm at all. She was helpless and feeble. Her rights were stolen from her. She was robbed of her very will, smashed of her soul. She was being overpowered in her affairs, despised in her place and dependent upon others with respect to her own dealings.

The Roman husband could have her put to death whenever he willed. The Greeks considered her a piece of chattel. The Jews despised her and considered her something impure. Worse than that, the Christians were at a loss concerning her, not knowing if she were a human with a soul or without a soul. Finally, she was taken as a bad omen, which resulted in female infanticide, as was the case with the Arabs in the Days of Ignorance.

After ail of that misery that she had to taste and all of that suffering that her heart had to swallow throughout the ages, the light of Islam shined and established the balance of truth for the honor of women. Islam gave her all of her rights, without any shortcoming. It taised her being from the shackles of disgrace that she had been facing throughout history. Islam announced her complete humanness and her qualifications for complete rights. It also protected her from being the plaything of desires and the trial of being simply a source of sexual, animalistic enjoyment. It made her an active component in furthering the society, in holding it together and keeping it wholesome.

The fundamental reforms and shareeah rights brought about by Islam included her receiving rights over her husband, in the same way that he has rights over her. These rights also include:

The dower: This is property exclusively for her possession and is one of her rights, as a token of respect for her and as a symbol of her worth and indispensability. It does not mean that she is a piece of merchandise that one buys. Instead, it is a symbol of honor and esteem.

‘The Figh of Marriage

It is also a sign of affection and a builder of the ties of love and mercy. It is also something that takes into consideration the woman’s natural desire for possessions and adornments. It is also a clear sign of the husband’s determination to bear the responsibilities of marriage and to fulfill her rights. It is like a first step in the many practical steps to improve the situation of the wife and to make her realize her place in society and to make her realize that [as a wife] she is something sought after and wanted.
content_id:
Fiqh Pernikahan dalam Tinjauan Quran dan Sunnah

Mencakup Mahar, Malam Pernikahan, Walimah, serta Hak-Hak Suami dan Istri

Dr. Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan Profesor, Fakultas Syariah Universitas Islam Muhammad ibn Saud Riyadh, Arab Saudi

Diterjemahkan oleh Jamaal al-Din M. Zarabozo

Dicetak atas Biaya Almarhum Qasim Darwish Fakhrou

Fiqh Pernikahan

PENGANTAR EDISI KEDUA

PENGANTAR [EDISI PERTAMA] BUKU INI

JENIS-JENIS HAK ISTRI ATAS SUAMINYA. HUKUM MENYEBUTKAN MAHAR PADA SAAT AKAD NIKAH. KEDUDUKAN MAHAR. JUMLAH MAKSIMAL MAHAR YANG DIPERBOLEHKAN. JUMLAH MINIMAL YANG DIPERBOLEHKAN UNTUK MAHAR. MAHAR BERLEBIHAN: SEBAB, AKIBAT, DAN HUKUMNYA. KAPAN WANITA BERHAK ATAS SELURUH MAHARNYA. PEMBAYARAN SETENGAH MAHAR.

PELAKSANAAN AKAD NIKAH ... KASUS-KASUS DI MANA LAKI-LAKI TIDAK WAJIB MEMBAYAR BAGIAN APA PUN DARI MAHAR

HUKUM PEMBAYARAN MAHAR SECARA TUNAI ATAU BERTAHAP... HUKUM MENGENAI SEBAGIAN MAHAR YANG DIBAYAR DI MUKA ATAU HADIAH YANG DIBERIKAN KEPADA PENGANTIN WANITA DALAM KASUS DI MANA LAKI-LAKI MEMUTUSKAN UNTUK TIDAK

MENIKAHI WANITA TERSEBUT.. CINCIN PERTUNANGAN: HUKUMNYA BAGI LAKI-LAKI MAUPUN WANITA

DUA: MENGUMUMKAN PERNIKAHAN DAN MEMBERITAHUKANNYA ..... 69

HUKUM MEMBERITAHUKAN PERNIKAHAN. MAKNA MENGUMUMKAN PERNIKAHAN...

Fiqh Pernikahan

TIGA: WALIMAH (PESTA PERNIKAHAN) DAN NASIHAT MENGENAI PEMBOROSAN DALAM PESTA PERNIKAHAN ....

ASAL KATA WALIMAH, DEFINISI SECARA BAHASA DAN ISTILAH.

EMPAT: UCAPAN SELAMAT ATAS PERNIKAHAN DAN DU'A UNTUK PASANGAN...

HIKMAH DI BALIK DISYARIATKANNYA UCAPAN SELAMAT... APA YANG HARUS DIUCAPKAN OLEH ORANG YANG MENGUCAPKAN SELAMAT KEPADA SAUDARANYA. BULAN MADU

LIMA: PERINGATAN DAN SOLUSI SEBAGAIMANA YANG SAYA LIHAT

ENAM: ADAB MALAM PERNIKAHAN DAN KEHIDUPAN SUAMI ISTRI

Fiqh Pernikahan

Dengan nama Allah, Maha Pengasih, Maha Penyayang

Segala puji bagi Allah; kami memuji-Nya; kami memohon pertolongan-Nya; kami memohon ampunan-Nya; dan kami memohon petunjuk-Nya. Kami berlindung kepada Allah dari keburukan jiwa kami dan kejelekkan amal perbuatan kami. Barangsiapa yang Allah beri petunjuk, maka tidak ada yang dapat menyesatkannya. Dan barangsiapa yang Allah biarkan tersesat, maka tidak ada yang dapat memberinya petunjuk. Aku bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak disembah kecuali Allah, yang tidak memiliki sekutu. Dan aku bersaksi bahwa Muhammad adalah hamba dan Rasul-Nya.

Mayoritas besar kaum muslimin akan menikah pada suatu waktu dalam hidup mereka, jika mereka belum melakukannya. Pernikahan sudah pasti merupakan salah satu peristiwa terpenting yang terjadi dalam kehidupan pribadi seseorang. Namun, seperti semua aspek kehidupan lainnya, seseorang harus berhati-hati untuk mengikuti langkah-langkah yang benar dalam hal pernikahan. Seseorang harus dibimbing oleh hukum-hukum Allah dalam hal lamaran, melihat calon pengantin wanita, akad nikah, upacara pernikahan, mahar, dan sebagainya. Terlalu sering persoalan-persoalan ini tidak diatur oleh hukum Islam melainkan oleh adat kebiasaan masyarakat yang berlaku. Adat kebiasaan telah menjadi standar sedemikian rupa sehingga banyak orang tidak menyadari bahwa sebagian adat tersebut bertentangan dengan apa yang telah ditetapkan Quran dan Sunnah sebagai petunjuk bagi umat manusia. Situasinya terkadang memburuk hingga ketika orang-orang diberitahu tentang ajaran Islam yang sesungguhnya mengenai pernikahan, mereka menolaknya sebagai sesuatu yang asing dan tidak sesuai dengan apa yang mereka kenal sebagai "Islam."

Sudah menjadi keharusan bagi seluruh kaum muslimin untuk kembali kepada Quran, Sunnah, dan fiqh Islam untuk membimbing mereka dalam persoalan-persoalan yang berkaitan dengan pernikahan. Setiap aspek kehidupan seseorang harus dipandu oleh wahyu dan petunjuk yang datang dari Allah. Hal ini berlaku untuk segala hal, mulai dari cara pemerintah menjalankan roda pemerintahan hingga bagaimana seseorang memperlakukan suami atau istrinya sendiri. Tidak ada perbuatan

Fiqh Pernikahan

yang luput dari pengawasan Allah dan, oleh karena itu, tidak ada perbuatan yang berada di luar petunjuk agama-Nya.

Dr. Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan (semoga Allah membalasnya dan memelihara hidupnya) telah berupaya keras untuk menghilangkan banyak kesalahpahaman mengenai kehidupan pernikahan dalam Islam. Beliau telah menulis tiga karya kecil namun sangat baik yang mencakup beberapa aspek terpenting dari pernikahan. Salah satu karya tersebut membahas tentang perselisihan dalam rumah tangga (al-nusyuz). Karya kedua, yang edisi barunya sedang dipersiapkan, membahas tentang "perjanjian pranikah" atau syarat-syarat dalam akad nikah. Karya ketiga, yang sekarang ada di hadapan pembaca, merupakan pembahasan yang lebih umum tentang aspek-aspek dasar mahar, upacara pernikahan dan walimah, serta hak-hak suami dan istri. Diharapkan karya-karya ini akan memungkinkan kaum muslimin untuk memahami dan mengamalkan petunjuk Islam yang berkaitan dengan pernikahan. Dengan melakukan hal tersebut, mereka akan mengarahkan diri mereka, dengan karunia dan rahmat Allah, menuju cara hidup terbaik di dunia ini dan menuju ridha Allah di akhirat.

Saya ingin menyampaikan terima kasih secara pribadi kepada Syaikh Saalih al-Sadlaan atas izin yang diberikan kepada kami untuk menerjemahkan karya ini dan karya-karya beliau yang lain. Saya juga ingin berterima kasih kepada Br. Homaidan al-Turki atas upayanya yang tanpa henti dalam menerbitkan karya-karya ini. Saya juga harus berterima kasih kepada seorang saudari, yang lebih memilih untuk tidak disebutkan namanya, yang telah menyunting dan memberikan komentar atas naskah ini. Dan yang terakhir namun tidak kalah pentingnya, saya ingin berterima kasih kepada istri tercinta atas dukungan, bantuan, dan kesabarannya yang terus-menerus. Semoga Allah membalas mereka semua dengan balasan yang besar di kehidupan ini dan di akhirat.

Penerjemah, Jamaal al-Din M. Zarabozo

Boulder, CO 25 Januari 1999

Fiqh Pernikahan

Pengantar Edisi Kedua

Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah menciptakan untuk kita pasangan-pasangan dari diri kita sendiri dan telah menjadikan di antara kita rasa cinta dan kasih sayang. Itu adalah salah satu tanda-tanda kebesaran-Nya.

Shalawat dan salam semoga tercurah kepada orang yang kepadanya Tuhannya berfirman, "Kami telah mengutus rasul-rasul sebelummu dan Kami menjadikan untuk mereka istri-istri dan keturunan" (al-Ra'd 38), Nabi kita Muhammad. Semoga shalawat yang terbaik dan salam yang paling sempurna tercurah atasnya.

Karena persoalan-persoalan yang berkaitan dengan kehidupan pernikahan, walimah, dan konsumasi termasuk persoalan-persoalan yang membutuhkan penjelasan dengan dalil-dalil syariah, saya merasa baik untuk menulis tentang apa yang harus diketahui setiap muslim mengenai hal-hal tersebut.

Saya telah menggabungkan dua buku saya dalam penelitian yang ada di hadapan Anda ini. Kedua karya tersebut membahas tentang mahar, walimah, adab-adab yang berkaitan dengan malam pernikahan, dan hak-hak wajib masing-masing pasangan. Saya juga telah menambahkan pembahasan yang menjelaskan beberapa aspek negatif dan kesalahan-kesalahan yang dilakukan sebagian orang berkaitan dengan topik-topik tersebut.

Ini adalah edisi kedua buku ini. Koreksi-koreksi yang diperlukan pada edisi pertama telah dilakukan. Perhatian juga diberikan untuk membahas sumber-sumber hadith dan untuk memperjelas hal-hal yang perlu diperjelas. Selain itu, beberapa topik baru juga dibahas.

Ini adalah usaha saya dan Allah-lah yang dimohon untuk menjadikannya bermanfaat ketika kembali kepada-Nya. Dan Dialah yang dimohon untuk

1 Kami telah menulis dua buku lain yang berkaitan dengan persoalan pernikahan. Buku-buku tersebut adalah al-Syuruth al-Nikaah ("Syarat-Syarat dalam Akad Nikah"), yang diselesaikan pada tahun 1391 H dan edisi keduanya sedang dipersiapkan, dan al-Nusyuz ("Perselisihan dalam Rumah Tangga") yang diselesaikan pada tahun 1412 H. [Dengan karunia dan rahmat Allah, al-Nusyuz telah diterjemahkan ke dalam bahasa Inggris dan diterbitkan oleh al-Basheer Company for Publications and Translations. Karya lainnya, al-Syuruth, adalah proyek masa depan, insya Allah, dari al-Basheer Company—JZ]

Fiqh Pernikahan

menjadikannya di timbangan amal kebaikan saya pada hari di mana harta dan anak-anak tidak berguna bagi siapa pun kecuali orang yang datang kepada Allah dengan hati yang bersih. Semoga shalawat dan salam tercurah atas Nabi kita Muhammad, keluarganya, dan seluruh para Sahabatnya.

Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan

(semoga Allah mengampuninya, kedua orang tuanya, dan seluruh kaum muslimin)

Fiqh Pernikahan

Pengantar [Edisi Pertama] Buku Ini

Telah datang atas wanita suatu masa di mana keadaannya tidak tenteram. Urusannya tidak tenang sama sekali. Dia tidak berdaya dan lemah. Hak-haknya dirampas darinya. Dia dirampas dari kehendaknya sendiri, jiwanya dihancurkan. Dia dikuasai dalam urusannya, dihinakan di tempatnya, dan bergantung pada orang lain dalam urusan-urusannya sendiri.

Suami Romawi dapat menghukum mati istrinya kapan pun dia mau. Orang-orang Yunani menganggapnya sebagai barang milik. Orang-orang Yahudi menghinanya dan menganggapnya sebagai sesuatu yang najis. Lebih buruk dari itu, orang-orang Kristen kebingungan mengenai dirinya, tidak mengetahui apakah dia manusia yang memiliki jiwa atau tidak memiliki jiwa. Akhirnya, dia dianggap sebagai pertanda buruk, yang mengakibatkan pembunuhan bayi perempuan, sebagaimana yang terjadi pada bangsa Arab di masa Jahiliyyah.

Setelah semua kesengsaraan yang harus dia rasakan dan semua penderitaan yang harus ditelan hatinya sepanjang masa, cahaya Islam bersinar dan menegakkan timbangan kebenaran untuk kehormatan wanita. Islam memberikannya semua haknya, tanpa kekurangan sedikit pun. Islam mengangkat keberadaannya dari belenggu kehinaan yang telah dia hadapi sepanjang sejarah. Islam mengumumkan kemanusiaannya yang sempurna dan kelayakannya untuk mendapatkan hak-hak yang lengkap. Islam juga melindunginya dari dijadikan mainan nafsu dan ujian berupa sekadar sumber kenikmatan seksual yang bersifat kebinatangan. Islam menjadikannya komponen aktif dalam memajukan masyarakat, menyatukannya, dan menjaganya tetap sehat.

Reformasi mendasar dan hak-hak syariah yang dibawa oleh Islam mencakup diterimanya hak-hak atas suaminya, sebagaimana suami memiliki hak-hak atasnya. Hak-hak ini juga meliputi:

Mahar: Ini adalah harta yang secara eksklusif menjadi miliknya dan merupakan salah satu haknya, sebagai tanda penghormatan kepadanya dan sebagai simbol nilai dan kebutuhannya yang tak tergantikan. Ini tidak berarti bahwa dia adalah barang dagangan yang dibeli. Sebaliknya, ini adalah simbol kehormatan dan penghargaan.

Fiqh Pernikahan

Ini juga merupakan tanda kasih sayang dan pembangun ikatan cinta dan rahmat. Ini juga sesuatu yang mempertimbangkan keinginan alami wanita terhadap harta benda dan perhiasan. Ini juga merupakan tanda nyata dari tekad suami untuk memikul tanggung jawab pernikahan dan memenuhi hak-hak istrinya. Ini seperti langkah pertama dalam banyak langkah praktis untuk memperbaiki keadaan istri dan membuatnya menyadari kedudukannya dalam masyarakat serta membuatnya menyadari bahwa [sebagai seorang istri] dia adalah sesuatu yang dicari dan diinginkan.
content_ar:
The الفقه of Marriage in the Light of the القرآن and السنة

Covering the Dower, Wedding Night, Wedding Feast and Rights of the Husband and Wife

Dr. Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan Professor, College of Shareeah Muhammad ibn Saud Islamic University Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

Translated by Jamaal al-Din M. Zarabozo

Printed at the Expense of the Late Qasim Darwish Fakhrou

‘The Figh of Marriage

INTRODUCTION TO THE SECOND EDITION ..ssscsssssessessssssssees

INTRODUCTION TO THE [FIRST EDITION] OF THE BOOK.

THE TYPES OF RIGHTS OF THE WIFE UPON HER HUSBAND. THE RULING CONCERNING STATING THE DOWER AT THE TIME OF THE MARRIAGE CONTRACT THE STATUS OF THE DOWER THE MAXIMUM AMOUNT OF DOWER ALLOWED THE MINIMUM AMOUNT ALLOWABLE FOR A DOWER. EXTRAVAGANT DOWERS: THE CAUSES, RESULTS AND RULING ‘WHEN THE WOMAN IS ENTITLED TO HER ENTIRE DOWER PAYING HALF OF THE DOWER.

CONTRACTING OF THE MARRIAGE ... CASES WHEREIN THE MAN IS NOT OBLIGED TO PAY ANY PORTION OF THE

THE RULING CONCERNING PAYING THE DOWER ON THE SPOT OR OVER TIME... THE RULING CONCERNING A PORTION OF THE DOWER PAID IN ADVANCE O ORA GIFT GIVEN TO THE BRIDE IN THE CASE WHERE THE MAN DECIDES NOT TO

MARRY THE WOMAN .. ENGAGEMENT RINGS: THEIR RULING FOR EITHER MEN OR WOMEN

TWO: ANNOUNCING THE MARRIAGE AND MAKING IT KNOWN . 69

THE RULING CONCERNING MAKING THE MARRIAGE KNOWN THE MEANING OF ANNOUNCING THE MARRIAGE...

‘The Figh of Marriage

THREE: THE WEDDING DINNER (AZ-WALEEMAH) AND AN ADMONITION CONCERNING EXTRAVAGANCE AT WEDDING PARTIES ....

THE ROOT OF THE WORD WALEEMAH, ITS LINGUISTIC AND TECHNICAL DEFINITION.

FOUR: CONGRATULATIONS UPON MARRIAGE AND SUPPLICATIONS FOR THE SPOUSE...

THE WISDOM BEHIND THE SANCTIONING OF CONGRATULATIONS... WHAT THE CONGRATULATING PERSON SHOULD SAY TO HIS BROTHER. THE HONEYMOON

FIVE: A WARNING AND A CURE ASI SEE IT

SIX: ETIQUETTE OF THE WEDDING NIGHT AND MARITAL

The Figh of Marriage

Tn the name of الله, Most Compassionate, Most Merciful

All praises are due to الله; we praise Him; we seek His help; we seek His forgiveness; and we seek His guidance. We seek refuge in الله from the evil in our souls and the badness of our deeds. For whomever الله guides, there is none to lead him astray. And for whomever He allows to go astray, there is none to guide him. I bear witness that there is none worthy of worship except الله, for whom there is no partner. And I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger.

The vast majority of المسلمين will get married at one time or another in their lives, if they have not already done so. Marriage is definitely one of the most important events that takes place in one’s private life. However, like all other aspects of one’s life, one must be careful to follow the proper steps when it comes to marriage. One must be guided by the laws of الله when it comes to the marriage proposal, seeing the prospective bride, the marriage contract, the marriage ceremony, the dower and so forth. Too many times these issues are ruled not by Islamic law but by the prevalent customs of the people. The customs have become the standard to such an extent that many people are not aware that some of those customs are in contradiction with what the القرآن and السنة have laid down as guidance for mankind. The situation has sometimes deteriorated to the point that when people are told of the true teachings of الإسلام concerning marriage, they reject it as something foreign and alien to what they have come to know as “الإسلام.”

It is imperative that all المسلمين return to the القرآن, السنة and Islamic figh to guide them in matters related to marriage. Every aspect of a person’s life should be guided by the revelation and guidance that has come from الله. This is true for everything from the way the government runs to how a person treats his or her own spouse. No action

‘The Figh of Marriage

falls outside of the watchful view of الله and, hence, no action falls outside of the guidance of His religion.

Dr. Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan (may الله reward him and preserve his life) has striven to remove much of the mistaken notions concerning married life in الإسلام. He has written three small but excellent works that cover some of the most important aspects of marriage. One of these works concerns marital discord (al-nushooz). A second work, of which a new edition is currently being prepared, deals with “pre-nuptial contracts” or conditions in the marriage contract. The third work, which is now before the reader, is a more general discussion of the basic aspects of the dower, the marriage ceremony and wedding party, and the rights of the husband and the wife. It is hoped that these works will allow المسلمين to understand and implement the guidance of الإسلام related to marriage. In doing so, they will lead themselves by the grace and mercy of الله to the best way of life in this world and to الله’s pleasure in the Hereafter.

I would like to express my personal thanks to Shaikh Saalih alSadlaan for giving us the permission to translate this work and his other works. I would also like to thank Br. Homaidan al-Turki for his relentless efforts in getting these works published. I must also the sister, who prefers to remain anonymous, who edited and commented upon the manuscript. And last but not least, I would like to thank my beloved wife for her continual support, help and patience. May الله reward all of them with a great reward in both this life and the Hereafter.

The translator, Jamaal al-Din M. Zarabozo

Boulder, CO January 25, 1999

The Figh of Marriage

Introduction to the Second Edition

All praise be to الله who created for us spouses from our own selves and has placed between us love and mercy. That is one of His great signs.

Peace and blessings be upon the one to whom his Lord said, “We have sent messengers before you and We made for them wives and offspring” (a/-Raad 38), our Prophet Muhammad. Upon him be the best of blessings and most complete peace.

Since the issues related to married life, wedding feasts and consummation are among those issues for which people need explanations with shareeah evidence, I thought it good to write about what every المسلمين should know concerning these matters.!

I have joined together two of my books in this research that is in front of you. Those works covered the dower, wedding party, manners related to the night of the wedding and the obligatory rights of each spouse. I have also added discussions clarifying some of the negative aspects and mistakes that some people commit concerning those topics.

This is the second edition of this book. The necessary corrections to the first edition were made. Care was also taken to discuss the sources of the الحديث and to clarify matters that needed to be clarified. In addition, some new topics were discussed.

This is my striving and الله is the one to be asked to make it beneficial when it goes back to Him. And He is the one to be asked to

1 We have written two other books related to marital issues. These are al-Shuroot al-Nikaab (“Conditions in the Marriage Contract”), which was completed in 1391 AH. and for which a second edition is under preparation, and al-Nushooz (“Marital Discord”) which was completed in 1412 AH. [By the race and mercy of الله, alNushooz has been translated into English and published by al-Basheer Company for Publications and Translations. The other work, al-Shuroot, is a future project, الله willing, of al-Basheer Company—JZ]

‘The Fight of Marriage

make it on the scales of my good deeds on the day in which wealth and children will not avail anyone except for the one who comes to الله with a pure heart. And may the peace and blessings be upon our Prophet Muhammad, his family and all of his Companions.

Saalih ibn Ghaanim al-Sadlaan

(may الله pardon him, his parents and all المسلمين)

‘The Fight of Marriage

Introduction to the [First Edition! of the Book

There came over woman a period of time in which her situation was restless. Her affairs were not calm at all. She was helpless and feeble. Her rights were stolen from her. She was robbed of her very will, smashed of her soul. She was being overpowered in her affairs, despised in her place and dependent upon others with respect to her own dealings.

The Roman husband could have her put to death whenever he willed. The Greeks considered her a piece of chattel. The Jews despised her and considered her something impure. Worse than that, the Christians were at a loss concerning her, not knowing if she were a human with a soul or without a soul. Finally, she was taken as a bad omen, which resulted in female infanticide, as was the case with the Arabs in the Days of Ignorance.

After ail of that misery that she had to taste and all of that suffering that her heart had to swallow throughout the ages, the light of الإسلام shined and established the balance of truth for the honor of women. الإسلام gave her all of her rights, without any shortcoming. It taised her being from the shackles of disgrace that she had been facing throughout history. الإسلام announced her complete humanness and her qualifications for complete rights. It also protected her from being the plaything of desires and the trial of being simply a source of sexual, animalistic enjoyment. It made her an active component in furthering the society, in holding it together and keeping it wholesome.

The fundamental reforms and shareeah rights brought about by الإسلام included her receiving rights over her husband, in the same way that he has rights over her. These rights also include:

The dower: This is property exclusively for her possession and is one of her rights, as a token of respect for her and as a symbol of her worth and indispensability. It does not mean that she is a piece of merchandise that one buys. Instead, it is a symbol of honor and esteem.

‘The Figh of Marriage

It is also a sign of affection and a builder of the ties of love and mercy. It is also something that takes into consideration the woman’s natural desire for possessions and adornments. It is also a clear sign of the husband’s determination to bear the responsibilities of marriage and to fulfill her rights. It is like a first step in the many practical steps to improve the situation of the wife and to make her realize her place in society and to make her realize that [as a wife] she is something sought after and wanted.
